five_questions.problem: The scarcity of robotic data poses a significant bottleneck in developing humanoid foundation models.
five_questions.hard: Bridging the cross-embodiment chasm remains a fundamental challenge due to kinematic mismatches.
five_questions.tried: UniT (Unified Latent Action Tokenizer via Visual Anchoring) was developed to address this challenge.
five_questions.outcomes: UniT achieves state-of-the-art data efficiency and robust out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization on both humanoid simulation benchmarks and real-world deployments.
five_questions.next: Potential remedies to address UniT's limitations include developing alternative methods for bridging the cross-embodiment chasm and exploring other scalable alternatives to robotic data for humanoid foundation models.

grounding_issues: UniT achieves 85% success rate on Humanoid Simulation benchmark
UniT achieves 90% success rate on Real-world Deployments
Baseline performance on Humanoid Simulation benchmark is 60% success rate
Baseline performance on Real-world Deployments is 70% success rate

analyst_claims: VLA-UniT predicts unified tokens to leverage diverse human data for policy learning.
VLA-UniT achieves robust out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization and zero-shot task transfer on humanoid robots.
UniT demonstrates state-of-the-art data efficiency on humanoid simulation benchmarks and real-world deployments.
UniT is validated across two paradigms: policy learning and world modeling.
VLA-UniT achieves high success rates in tasks like Pick and Place Articulated on RoboCasa GR1 simulation environments.
VLA-UniT outperforms baselines such as Diffusion Policy, Flare, and GROOT in terms of success rate and data efficiency.

rewrite_targets: What would falsify UniT's main claim?

review_notes.summary: The draft lacks concrete experiments to falsify UniT's claims and makes vague impact claims about its potential extensions.
